message:
fix(crates): protocol flag validation, sandbox zero-value rejection, and telemetry init race protection

fcp-protocol/fcps.rs — mutually exclusive flag validation:
- FcpsFrameHeader::decode now rejects mutually exclusive flag
  combinations at parse time: ERROR+RESPONSE (a frame cannot be both an
  error and a successful response) and STREAM_END without STREAMING (end
  of stream is meaningless if the frame was not part of a stream).
- Add InvalidFlags variant to FrameError with a reason string.
- FcpsFrame::encode now returns Result<Vec<u8>, FrameError> instead of
  panicking on header/payload length mismatch. The debug_assert was
  previously the only guard — callers constructing frames with mismatched
  header.total_payload_len could produce corrupt wire bytes in release
  builds.

fcp-sandbox/sandbox.rs — zero-value resource limit rejection:
- CompiledPolicy::from_manifest now validates that memory_mb,
  wall_clock_timeout_ms, and cpu_percent are all > 0 before compiling
  the sandbox policy. A zero value in any of these makes execution
  impossible, and the old code would silently produce a policy that
  caused immediate OOM-kill (memory), instant timeout (wall_clock), or
  zero-CPU starvation (cpu_percent).
- Also validates cpu_percent <= 100.
- Update test_compiled_policy_zero_memory to expect InvalidConfig error
  instead of silently accepting 0.
- Add test_compiled_policy_zero_cpu and test for cpu_percent > 100.

fcp-telemetry/lib.rs — double-checked locking for init:
- Replace the unsafe racy OnceLock::set pattern with a Mutex-guarded
  double-checked lock (TELEMETRY_INIT_LOCK). The old code had a TOCTOU
  race: two threads could both pass the OnceLock::get().is_none() check,
  both call init_logging (which registers a global tracing subscriber),
  and the second call would fail.
- Extract init_telemetry_with() that accepts injectable init functions
  for logging, prometheus, and OTLP — enabling unit tests to verify
  initialization logic without touching global tracing state.

fcp-mesh/gossip.rs — minor gossip protocol addition.

.rchignore — exclude codex recovery/snapshot dirs and recovery-promotions
from rch file transfers to prevent rsync overflow on large beads trees.

README.md — document .rch/config.toml purpose and add troubleshooting
entry for rsync overflow on .beads/recovery_* during artifact retrieval.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1441,6 +1441,8 @@ FCP integrates with the broader Agent Flywheel ecosystem:
 In shared multi-agent sessions, offload CPU-heavy Cargo work through `rch` so local machines do not
 turn into compilation bottlenecks. `rch` fails open to local execution if the worker fleet is unavailable; in swarm-style sessions, abort that local fallback instead of letting heavy Cargo work pile onto the workstation unexpectedly.
 
+This repo also carries a project-level [`.rch/config.toml`](/Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/.rch/config.toml) that pins the critical `.beads` transfer excludes in effective `rch` config. If `rch` prints `Remote command finished: exit=0` and only then fails during `Retrieving build artifacts...`, treat that as a tooling or worker-state problem rather than a Cargo failure; keep the retrieval stderr, then retry on a different worker or continue the investigation in `rch` itself.
+
 ```bash
 # Build the default workspace members (core platform crates)
 rch exec -- cargo build --release
@@ -1793,6 +1795,7 @@ Honest about what FCP doesn't do yet:
 | `fwc list` returns "missing-host-endpoint" | No `fcp-host` running | Use `fwc list --offline` for workspace manifest data |
 | Connector returns `NotConfigured` | `configure` not called before `invoke` | Call `fwc config schema <connector>` to see required params, then configure |
 | `cargo build` OOMs on macOS | Too many parallel codegen units | Set `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-build` to avoid Cargo lock contention; or use `rch exec -- cargo build` to offload to remote workers |
+| `rch exec -- cargo ...` ends with rsync `overflow ... .beads/recovery_*` / `recv_file_entry` / code 22 | The remote Cargo command may already have succeeded, but artifact retrieval is walking a worker-side `.beads` recovery tree that current excludes did not prune from that worker | Read the remote-command status lines first. If `rch` reports a remote `exit=0`, treat the compile/test itself as successful and the failure as tooling state. This repo mirrors the critical excludes in both `.rchignore` and [`.rch/config.toml`](/Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/.rch/config.toml), but retrieval-side pruning still needs an upstream `rch` fix or worker cleanup. |
 | Clippy fails with `fcp-async-core` errors | Pre-existing lints in async-core test code | These are upstream; connector code is clean. Run clippy on specific crates: `cargo clippy -p fcp-<crate>` |
 | OAuth token refresh fails | Token expired between materialize and use | For `credential_id` auth, the egress proxy handles refresh. For `access_token`, re-run configure with a fresh token |
 | SSE stream stops without error | Connection idle timeout | The Anthropic SSE parser has a 16 MiB buffer limit and proper CRLF handling. Check network/proxy timeout settings |
